devdoc.details: Architecture:  1. `initializeMarket()` prepares a CTF condition and emits `MarketInitialized` (triggers CRE).  2. CRE detects the event, calls Gemini AI, then submits `onReport(metadata, report)`.  3. The adapter starts a `disputeWindow` timer and emits `ResolutionProposed`.  4. After the window, anyone calls `finalizeResolution()` to call `ctf.reportPayouts()`.  5. Before the window elapses (public finalize):     - The contract owner can `adminOverride()` to replace the proposed payouts.     - The multisig (if set) can call `multisigOverride()` which requires M-of-N signers.  6. Owner / multisig bypass (no need to wait for CRE or resolution time):     - `adminResolve()` — owner sets payouts and resolves immediately (Active or Proposed, any time).     - `adminOverride()` — also allowed while Active to start a proposal + dispute window without CRE.     - `multisigOverride()` — signers may begin or complete resolution while Active (first approvals       move the market to Proposed and open the usual dispute window for public finalize). Key invariants:  - AiCTFAdapter is the sole oracle address in every `prepareCondition()` call.  - No market string metadata is stored on-chain — only the IPFS CID via events.  - Permissionless `finalizeResolution` still requires the dispute window to elapse after a proposal.

OwnableInvalidOwner(address).details: The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)

OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address).details: The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.

adminOverride(bytes32,uint256[]).details: While Active: sets status to Proposed and opens the dispute window (no CRE required).      While Proposed: only callable before the dispute window ends (same as before).

params.payouts: Payout array for the new proposal.

adminResolve(bytes32,uint256[]).details: Works while market is Active (before CRE) or Proposed. Bypasses resolution time,      dispute window, and CRE. Does not require a prior AI proposal.

params.payouts: Payout numerators per outcome (same semantics as CTF `reportPayouts`).

params._ctf: Address of the deployed ConditionalTokens contract.
params._disputeWindow: Seconds after a proposal before it can be finalized.
params._forwarderAddress: Chainlink CRE forwarder (only address allowed to call onReport).
params._multisigSigners: Initial list of multisig signer addresses.

finalizeResolution(bytes32).details: Permissionless — anyone can call once the window is over.

initializeMarket(bytes32,string,uint256,.details: Only callable by the MarketRegistry contract.      Emits MarketInitialized — this event is the CRE log trigger.

params.ipfsCid: IPFS CID of the JSON metadata — NOT stored on-chain, emitted only.
params.outcomeCount: Number of outcome slots (2 for binary markets).
params.resolutionTime: Unix timestamp when CRE should attempt resolution.

multisigOverride(bytes32,uint256[]).details: Once `multisigThreshold` approvals are collected, the resolution is finalized      immediately — bypassing the remaining dispute window.      While Active: signers pin payouts, the market moves to Proposed, then approvals accrue;      meeting threshold finalizes immediately (same as after a CRE proposal).      While Proposed: the dispute window must still be open (same as before).

params.payouts: The new payout array all signers are approving.                    All signers must supply identical payouts; any mismatch resets approvals.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[EIP section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

devdoc.title: AiCTFAdapter

adminOverride(bytes32,uint256[]).notice: Owner replaces payouts and (re)starts the dispute window without resolving.

adminResolve(bytes32,uint256[]).notice: Owner may resolve immediately with the given payouts.

ctf().notice: The Gnosis Conditional Token Framework contract

disputeWindow().notice: Duration of the dispute window after a resolution is proposed

finalizeResolution(bytes32).notice: Finalizes a proposed resolution after the dispute window elapses.

initializeMarket(bytes32,string,uint256,.notice: Registers a new prediction market with the CTF and Chainlink CRE.

multisigApprovalCount(bytes32).notice: questionId → current multisig approval count for a proposed override

multisigApprovals(bytes32,address).notice: questionId → signer → has approved the pending proposal override

multisigOverride(bytes32,uint256[]).notice: A multisig signer approves the current pending proposal override.

multisigThreshold().notice: Minimum number of multisig signers required to approve an override

registry().notice: Address of the MarketRegistry (only it may call initializeMarket)

setRegistry(address).notice: Only the deployer (owner) can set the registry address.

userdoc.notice: Oracle adapter that resolves Conditional Token Framework markets via Chainlink CRE + Gemini AI.
